body.value: IN CIUITATE LINCOLIA. erant Tempore regis Eduuardi nouies centum et LXX. mansiones hospitatae. Hic numerus anglice computatur. I. Centum pro. C. tum XX. ti
In the City of LINCOLN there were 970 inhabited messuages before 1066. This number is reckoned by the English method, 100 for 120.

In ipsa ciuitate erant. XII. Lageman. idest habentes sacam et socam. Hardecnut. Suartin. filius. grimboldi. Ulf filius Suertebrand. qui habuit thol et theim. Walrauen. Aluuold. Britric. Guret. Ulbert. Godric. filius. eddeue. Siuuard presbyter. Leuuine presbyter. Aldene presbyter.

In the City itself there were 12 lawmen, that is those who have full jurisdiction: Harthacnut; Swarting son of Grimbald; Ulf 's son, Swartbrand, who had market rights; Walraven ; Alwold; Brictric; Guthroth; Wulfbert ; Godric son of Edeva; Siward the priest; Leofwin the priest; Healfdene the priest.

Modo sunt ibi totidem. habentes similiter sacam et socam. Suardinc. I. loco Hardecnut patris sui. Suartinc. II. . Sortebrand. III. loco Ulf patris sui. Agemund. IIII. loco Walrauen patris sui. Aluuold. V. . Goduinus filius Brictric. VI. . Normannus crassus VII. loco Guret. Ulbert frater Ulf VIII. adhuc uiuit. Petrus de ualonges IX. loco Godric filii Eddeue. Ulnodus. X. presbyter loco Siuuard. presbyter. Buruolt XI. loco patris sui Leuuine qui modo. est monachus. Leduuinus filius rauene. XII. loco Aldene presbyteri.

Now there are as many there who likewise have full jurisdiction: 1. Swarting in place of his father Harthacnut; 2. Swarting; 3. Swartbrand in place of his father Ulf ; 4. Aghmund in place of his father Walraven; 5. Alwold; 6. Godwin son of Brictric; 7. Norman the fat in place of Guthroth; 8. Wulfbert, Ulf's brother, is still living; 9. Peter of Valognes in place of Godric son of Edeva; 10. Wulfnoth the priest in place of Siward the priest; 11. Burgwald in place of his father Leofwin who is now a monk; 12. Leodwin son of Raven in place of Healfdene the priest.

Tochi filius Outi habuit in ciuitate. XXX. mansiones praeter suam Hallam. et II. aecclesias et dimidiam. et suam hallam habuit quietam ab omni consuetudine. et super alias XXX. mansiones habuit locationem. et praeter hoc de una quaque unum denarium idest landgable. Super has. XXX. mansiones habebat rex theloneum et forisfacturam. ut burgenses iurauerunt. Sed his iurantibus contradicit Uluiet presbyter. et offert se portaturum iudicium quod non ita est sicuti dicunt. Hanc aulam tenet Goisfridus alselin. et suus nepos Radulfus. Remigius episcopus tenet supradictas. XXX. mansiones in aecclesia SANCTAE MARIAE. ita quod Goisfridus alselin nichil inde habet. neque scangium neque aliam redditionem. Isdem Goisfridus habet. I. mansionem extra murum. unde habet landgable. sicut habuit Tochi.

Toki son of Auti had 30 messuages in the City besides his hall and 2 1/2 churches. He had his hall exempt from every customary due. He had rent in respect of the 30 other messuages, and besides this 1d from each one, that is the land tribute. In respect of these 30 messuages the King had toll and forfeiture, as the burgesses have sworn. But Wulfgeat the priest contradicts their sworn testimony and offers to undergo ordeal that it is not as they say. Geoffrey Alselin and his nephew Ralph hold this hall. Bishop Remigius holds the said 30 messuages among the church of St Mary with the result that Geoffrey Alselin has nothing from them, neither exchange nor other payment. Geoffrey also has 1 messuage outside the wall from which he has the land tribute, as Toki had.
# Radulfus pagenel habet. I. mansionem quae fuit Merlesuen. quietam ab omni consuetudine.
Ralph Paynel has 1 messuage, which was Merleswein 's, exempt from every customary due.
# Ernuin presbyter habet. I. mansionem Morcari comitis cum saca et soca. et sic de rege tenet sicut Morcar habuit. ut ipse dicit.
Ernwin the priest has 1 messuage of Earl Morcar's with full jurisdiction; he holds from the King in the same way as Morcar had it, as he says himself.
# Gislebertus de gand habet. I. mansionem. Ulf. cum saca et soca. et aliam mansionem unde habebat. I. denarium. et iterum. I. mansionem. Siuuard quietam ab omni consuetudine.
Gilbert of Ghent has 1 messuage, Ulf 's, with full jurisdiction, another messuage from which he had 1d, and a further 1 messuage, Siward's, exempt from every customary due.
# Comes Hugo habet. I. mansionem comitis Heroldi. cum saca et soca. et II. mansiones unde habet landgable.
Earl Hugh has 1 messuage, Earl Harold's, with full jurisdiction, and 2 messuages from which he has the land tribute.
# Rogerius de busli habet. I. mansionem Suen. filii. Suaue. cum saca /et soca.
Roger of Bully has 1 messuage, Swein son of Svavi's, with full jurisdiction.
# Iudita comitissa. habet. I. mansionem Stori sine saca et soca. et hanc calumniatur Iuo taillebosc per burgenses.
Countess Judith has 1 messuage, Stori <of Bolingbroke>'s, without full jurisdiction. Ivo Tallboys claims it through the burgesses.

# Remigius episcopus habet. I. maneriolum cum. I. carucata in contiguum ciuitateti Lincolia. ae cum saca et soca et cum Thol et Theim. et super III. mansiiones similiter. et super. II. aecclesias. et super. LXXVIII. mansiones similiter. praeter geldum regis quod dant cum burgensibus. Ex his sunt. XX. mansiones Wastae. De tribus superioribus mansionibus est. I. quieta ab omnibus rebus.

Bishop Remigius has 1 small manor with 1 carucate adjacent to the City of LINCOLN, with full jurisdiction and market rights; likewise in respect of 3 messuages, 2 churches and 78 messuages except for the King's tax which they pay with the burgesses. Of these, 20 messuages are unoccupied. Of the 3 messuages above, 1 is exempt from everything, but 2 are taxed with the burgesses.

In campis Lincoliae extra sunt XII. carucatae terrae et dimidia. praeter carucatam episcopi ciuitatis. De hac terra habent Rex et comes. VIII. carucatas in dominio. Ex his dedit unam rex Willelmus cuidam Ulchel pro una naui quam ab eo emit. Ille uero qui nauim uendidit mortuus. est. et hanc carucatam terre nullus habet nisi rege concedente. Praeter has. VIII. carucatas. habet rex et comes CC et XXX. I. acras terrae arabilis inland. et C. acras prati.

In the fields of LINCOLN outside the City there are 12 1/2 carucates of land, besides the carucate of the Bishop of the City. Of this land the King and the Earl have 8 carucates in lordship. King William gave one of them to Ulfkil for a ship which he bought from him. But the man who sold the ship is dead and no one has this carucate of land except with the King's consent. Besides these 8 carucates the King and the Earl have 231 acres of arable land, , and 100 acres of meadow.
# De reliqua terra idest. IIII. carucatae et dimidia: T. R. E. habuit Ulf. I. carucatam. nunc habet filius eius Sortebrand.
Of the remaining land, that is 4 1/2 carucates, before 1066 Ulf had 1 carucate, now his son Swartbrand has it.

# Aliam carucatam. T. R. E. habuerunt Siuuard presbyter et Outi. et VI. acras terrae quas tenet Uluiet presbyter. Nunc habet Alfnod medietatem huius carucatae. et Norman filius Siuuard presbyteri alteram medietatem. Hanc uero praedictam medietatem istius terre. et uxorem Siuuardi presbyteri inuasit Unlof presbyter dum erat iN saisitione regis propter. XL. solidos quos ipsemet rex imposuerat super Siuuardum presbyterum.

Before 1066 Siward the priest and Auti had another carucate, and 6 acres of meadow, which Wulfgeat the priest holds. Now Alnoth has half of this carucate and Norman son of Siward the priest the other half. But Wulfnoth the priest sei
# Terciam carucatam habet Petrus de Ualongies. quam habuit Godricus T. R. E.
Peter of Valognes has the third carucate which Godric had before 1066.

# Quarta carucata adiacuit in aecclesia omnium sanctorum. T. R. E. et XII. toftes. et IIII. croftes. Hanc aecclesiam et terram aecclesiae et quicquid ad eam pertinuit. habuit Godricus filius Gareuinae. Sed eo facto monacho. abbas de Burg obtinet. Burgenses uero omnes Lincoliae dicunt quod iniuste habet. quia nec Gareuin nec Godricus filius eius nec ullus alius dare potuerunt extra ciuitatem nec extra parentes eorum. nisi concessu regis. Hanc aecclesiam et que ibi pertinet clamat Ernuin presbyter. . . hereditate Godrici consanguinei /sui.

The fourth carucate was attached to the church of All Saints before 1066, also 12 plots and 4 crofts. Godric son of Garwine had this church, the church's land and whatever belonged to it. But now that he has become a monk the Abbot of Peterborough holds them. But all the burgesses of LINCOLN say that he has them wrongfully, because neither Garwine nor Godric his son nor anyone else could grant them outside the City or outside their family except with the King's consent. This church and what belongs to it Ernwin the priest claims by inheritance from his kinsman Godric.
# Residuam dimidiam carucatam terrae habuit et habet SANCTA MARIA de Lincolia. in qua nunc est episcopatus.
St Mary's of Lincoln, where the Bishopric is now, had and has the remaining 1/2 carucate of land.
Inter aecclesias Lincoliae et burgenses. habuerunt. XXX. VI. croftes in Lincolia. exceptis. XII. et dimidia carucatis terrae quae |supra enumeratae sunt.
Between them the churches of Lincoln and the burgesses had 36 crofts in LINCOLN, besides the 12 1/2 carucates of land which are listed above.
# Ex praedictis mansionibus quae T. R. E. fuerunt hospitatae. sunt modo Waste. CC. anglico numero. I. CC. XL. et eodem numero septies centum et LX. sunt modo hospitatae.
Of the said messuages which were inhabited before 1066, 200 are now unoccupied by the English reckoning, that is 240, and by the same reckoning 760 are now inhabited.

# Hi subscripti non dederunt geldum regis sicut debuissent. Terra SANCTAE MARIE in qua Tedbertus manet in magno uico. non dedit geldum. neque terra episcopi ad Sanctum Laurentium posita. non dedit geldum de. I. domo. Abbas de burg de. I. domo et de. III. toftis non dedit geldum. Hugo comes de omni terra sua non dedit geldum. neque Turaldus de greteuilla. neque Losuardus neque Chetelbertus. Hugo. filius. Baldrici non dedit geldum de. II. toftis. Neque Goisfridus alselin. similiter de. II. toftis. Neque Gislebertus de. III. domibus dedit geldum. Neque Petrus de ualonges de sua domo. Neque iudita comitissa de sua domo. Neque Radulfus pagenel de. I. domo. Neque Radulfus de badpalmas de sua domo. Neque Ertaldus de sua domo.

Those written below have not paid the King's tax as they ought to have: The land of St Mary's where Theodbert lives in the High Street has not paid tax; nor has the Bishop's land situated at St Lawrence's paid tax on 1 house. The Abbot of Peterborough has not paid tax on 1 house and 3 plots. Earl Hugh has not paid tax on all his land, nor has Turold of Greetwell, nor Losoard <of Rolleston> nor Ketilbert. Hugh son of Baldric has not paid tax on 2 plots, nor has Geoffrey Alselin, likewise on 2 plots. Nor has Gilbert paid tax on 3 houses; nor Peter of Valognes on his house; nor Countess Judith on her house; nor Ralph Paynel on 1 house; nor Ralph of Bapaume on his house; nor Ertald on his house.

# Domum de qua abbas de Burg ut dictum. est non dedit geldum: ipsam clamat Normannus crassus de feuo regis. Ipsam enim habuit Godredus antecessor suus in uadimonio pro tribus markis argenti et dimidia.

The house on which the Abbot of Peterborough has not paid tax, as has been said , Norman the fat claims as of the King's Holding. For Guthroth his predecessor had it in pledge for 3 1/2 marks of silver.

# Colsuen habet in Lincolia ciuitate. IIII. toftes de terra Cole nepotis sui. et extra ciuitatem habet XXX. VI. domos et II. aecclesias in quibus nichil adiacet. quas hospitauit in Wasta terra quam rex sibi dedit. et quae nunquam ante hospitata fuit. Modo habet rex omnes consuetudines ex eis.

Kolsveinn has in the City of LINCOLN 4 plots of his nephew Cola's land. Outside the City he has 36 houses and 2 churches to which nothing is attached, which he settled on waste land which the King gave him and which had never been settled before. Now the King has all the customary dues from them.
# Aluredus nepos Turoldi habet. III. toftes de terra Sybi. quam rex sibi dedit. in quibus habet omnes consuetudines. praeter geldum regis de monedagio.
Alfred nephew of Turold, has 3 plots of Sibbi's land which the King gave him, in which he has all the customary dues except for the King's tax from the mint.
# Abbas de Elyg habet dimidiam mansionem de terra Edstan.
The Abbot of Ely has 1/2 messuage from .
# Hugo. filius. baldrici. habet. II. toftes quas rex sibi dedit.
Hugh son of Baldric has 2 plots which the King gave him.

De praedictis Wastis mansionibus. propter castellum destructae fuerunt C. LX. VI. Relique. LXX. IIII. Wastatae sunt extra metam castelli. non propter oppressionem uicomitum et ministrorum: sed propter infortunium et paupertatem. et ignium exustionem.

Of the said unoccupied messuages 166 were destroyed because of the castle; the remaining 74 are unoccupied outside the castle's perimeter, not because of the oppression of the Sheriffs and officers, but because of misfortune, poverty and the ravages of fire.
T. R. E. reddebat ciuitas Lincolia regi. XX. libras. et comiti. X. libras. Modo reddit. C. libras ad numerum. inter regem et comitem. Moneta uero. . . reddit. LXX. V. libras.
Before 1066 the City of LINCOLN paid £20 to the King and £10 to the Earl. Now it pays £100 at face value between the King and the Earl, but the mint pays £75.
Consuetudines regis et comitis in Sudlincolia: reddunt. XXVIII. libras.
The customary dues of the King and the Earl in South Lincoln pay £28.
In Norttreding consuetudines regis et comitis. reddunt. XX. IIII. libras.
In the North Riding the customary dues of the King and the Earl pay £24.
In Westreding consuetudines regis et comitis: reddunt. XII. libras.
In the West Riding the customary dues of the King and the Earl pay £12.
In Sudtreding consuetudines regis et comitis: reddunt. XV. libras.
In the South Riding the customary dues of the King and the Earl pay £15.
Pax manu regis uel Sigillo eius data. si fuerit infracta: emendatur per XVIII. hundrez. Unum quodque hundret soluit. VIII. libras. Duodecim hundrez emendant regi. et VI. comiti.
If the peace, given by the King's hand or by his seal, is broken, a fine is paid with 18 hundreds . Each hundred renders £8. 12 hundreds are the fine paid to the King and 6 to the Earl.
# Siquis pro aliquo reatu exulatus fuerit a rege et a comite et ab hominibus uicecomitatus: nullus nisi rex sibi dare pacem poterit.
If anyone for any crime should be exiled by the King, the Earl and the men of the Shire, no one but the King can give him peace. List 2

STANFORD BURGUM REGIS. dedit geldum T. R. E. pro. XII. hundrez et dimidio. In exercitu. et nauigio. et in Danegeld. Ibi fuerunt et sunt. VI. custodie. Quinque in Lincolescyre. et sexta in Hantunescyre. quae est ultra pontem. et tamen ipsa reddebat omnem consuetudinem cum aliis. praeter gablum et theloneum. quod abbas de Burg habebat et habet.

The King's Borough of STAMFORD paid tax before 1066 for 12 1/2 hundreds for the army, ship service and for Danegeld. There were and are six wards: five in Lincolnshire and the sixth in Northamptonshire - it is across the bridge; however, it paid every customary due with the other except for tribute and toll, which the Abbot of Peterborough had and has.
In his. V. custodiis T. R. E. fuerunt. CXL et I. mansiones. et dimidium molendinum. quae reddebant omnes consuetudines. Sed et modo totidem sunt. praeter. V. quae propter opus castri sunt wastae.

In these five wards before 1066 there were 141 messuages and 1/2 mill which paid all the customary dues; but now there are as many except for 5 which are unoccupied because of the construction of the castle.
In his custodiis sunt. VI. mansiones quae. T. R. E. dabant omnes consuetudines. modo autem non dant. Brand habet. IIII. or et Ulchetel filius Mereuuine. II. as
In these wards there are 6 messuages which before 1066 paid all the customary dues, but now they do not pay them. Brand has 4; Ulfkil son of Merewin, 2.

In his custodiis sunt LXVII. mansiones sochemanorum. qui habent terras suas in dominio. et qui petunt dominos ubi uolunt. super quos rex nichil aliud habet nisi emendationem foris facturae eorum. et heriete. et theloneum. et I. molinus de. XXX. solidis quod abstulit Eustacius de huntedune. Ilud erat unius sochemanni.

In these wards there are 77 messuages of Freemen who have their lands in lordship and seek lords where they will. The King has nothing over them except for a fine of their forfeiture, heriot and toll. 1 mill at 30s which Eustace of Huntingdon took away; it was one of the Freemen's.

In Stanford. T. R. E. erant. XII. Lagemanni. qui habebant infra domos suas sacam et socam. et super homines suos. praeter geld et heriete. et forisfacturam corporum suorum de XL. oris argenti. et preter Latronem. Hoc idem modo habent. Sed non sunt nisi nouem. Unus eorum habet XVII. mansiones sub se. et dimidium molinum. XV. solidorum. Alter. XIIII. cim mansiones. una ex his. est Wasta. Tercius. II. as mansiones. Quartus. II. as et dimidiam. Quintus. quinque Sextus. IIII. or Septimus. III. es Octauus. unam. Nonus. III. es sed Hugo musardus duas ei abstulit.

In STAMFORD before 1066 there were 12 lawmen who had full jurisdiction within their houses and over their men, except for tax, heriot, forfeiture involving their bodies at 40 of silver, and robbery. They also have this now, but they are only nine; one of them has 17 messuages under him and 1/2 mill, 15s; the second, 14 messuages - one of these is unoccupied; the third, 2 messuages; the fourth, 2 1/2; the fifth, 5; the sixth, 4; the seventh, 3; the eighth, 1; the ninth, 3, but Hugh Musard has taken 2 away from him.

In his custodiis adhuc sunt. XXII. mansiones. et II. aecclesiae cum. XII. acris terrae de. XIIII. solidis. quas habebat Ernuin presbyter. T. R. E. et Ezi habebat. I. mansionem. has. XX. III. mansiones habet modo Eudo dapifer. Super eas habebat rex omnem consuetudinem. modo non habet.

In these wards there are still 22 messuages and 2 churches with 12 acres of land at 14s which Ernwin the priest had before 1066; Edsi had 1 messuage. Eudo the steward now has these 23 messuages. The King had every customary due in respect of them, now he does not.
In eadem uilla habuit Azor. VII. mansiones et dimidium molinum. T. R. E. modo habet Gunfridus de cioches. Ad has pertinent. LXX. acrae extra uillam.
Also in this town A
# Eduuard cild habuit. XIIII. mansiones et LXX. acras. extra uillam. modo habet Iudita comitissa.
Edward the noble had 14 messuages and 70 acres outside the town; now Countess Judith has them.

# Edded regina habuit. LXX. mansiones quae iacuerunt in Roteland cum omnibus consuetudinibus. sine panificis. Ad has adiacent II. carucatae terrae et dimidia. et I. caruca arans. et XLV. acrae prati extra uillam. Modo habet Rex. Willelmus. et ualet VI. libras. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras.

Queen Edith had 70 messuages which lay in `Roteland', with all the customary dues, except the bakers'. Attached to them are 2 1/2 carucates of land and 1 plough, which is ploughing, and 45 acres of meadow outside the town. Now King William has them. Value £6; value before 1066 £4.
# Abbas de Burg habuit et habet in Stanford X. mansiones pertinentes ad Lincolesyre. et I. molinum. XL. solidorum. et V. solidos de domibus et de VIII. /acris.
In STAMFORD the Abbot of Peterborough had and has 10 messuages which belong to Lincolnshire; 1 mill, 40s; 5s from houses and from 8 acres.
# Leuuinus habuit. IX. mansiones. modo Aluredus habet. Item Leuuinus. I. mansionem ad omnem consuetudinem. sine geldo. quam modo habet Wido de renbudcurt.
Leofwin. Leofwin also 1 messuage with every customary due except tax, which Guy of Raimbeaucourt has now.
# Fastolf habuit unam aecclesiam de rege quietam cum. VIII. acris.
Fastulfr had 1 church from the King, exempt, with 8 acres.
# Albertus unam aecclesiam Sancti Petri cum. II. abus mansionibus. et dimidiam carucatam terre quae iacet in Rotelande in hemeldune. Ualet. X. solidos.
Albert 1 church, St Peter's , with 2 messuages and 1/2 carucate of land which lies in `Roteland', in Hambleton. Value 10s.
# Terram arabilem extra uillam in Lincolescire habet rex. DC. acras.
The King has 600 acres of arable land outside the town in Lincolnshire.
# Lagemanni et burgenses habent. CC. et LXXII. acras. sine omni consuetudine.
The lawmen and burgesses have 272 acres without every customary due.
# T. R. E. dabat Stanford. XV. libras. modo dat ad firmam. L. libras. De omni consuetudine regis. modo dat. XXVIII. libras.
Before 1066 STAMFORD paid £15, now it pays £50 in revenue. It now pays £28 in respect of every customary due of the King's. List 3

In TORCHESEY. T. R. E. fuerunt. CC. et XIII. burgenses. Easdem consuetudines habebant omnes quas et Lincolienses. et tantum plus quod quicunque eorum mansionem in eadem uilla habebat. neque intrans neque exiens theloneum dabat nec consuetudinem. Hoc autem eorum erat. ut si legati regis illuc uenirent: homines eiusdem uici cum nauibus suis et aliis instrumentis nauigationis usque eboracum eos conducerent. et uicecomes uictum legatorum et nautarum ex firma sua inueniret. Quod si aliquis burgensium alibi uellet abire. et domum quae esset in eadem uilla uendere: sine scientia |et licentia praepositi si uellet posset facere.

In TORKSEY before 1066 there were 213 burgesses. They all had the same customary dues as the men of Lincoln, and so much more, since whoever of them had a messuage in this town did not pay toll on entering or leaving nor a customary due. However, this was their : if the King's officers should come there the men of this small town should conduct them with their ships and other equipment for navigation as far as York, and the sheriff should find supplies for the officers and the sailors out of his revenue. But if any of the burgesses wished to go away anywhere and sell house which was in this town, he could do it, if he wished, without the knowledge and permission of the reeve.

Hoc suburbium Torchesyg et Harduic Manerium ei contiguum habuit Eddid regina in dominio. et habebat foris urbem. II. carucatas terrae sine geldo. Modo habet rex in dominio. et sunt ibi. CII. burgenses manentes. Wastae sunt uero. C. XI. mansiones. Ad hanc uillam pertinent XX. acrae prati. et LX. acrae siluae minutae. et XI. piscariae. Una ex his. est Berengarii de todeni. T. R. E. inter regem et comitem. ualuit. XVIII. libras. modo. XXX. libras.

Queen Edith had this small town of TORKSEY and the manor of Hardwick adjacent to it in lordship and she had 2 carucates of land without tax outside the town. Now the King has them in lordship; there are 102 burgesses who live there, but 111 messuages are unoccupied. To this town belong 20 acres of meadow, 60 acres of underwood and 11 fisheries. One of them is Berengar of Tosny's. Value before 1066, between the King and the Earl, £18; now £30.

T. R. E. reddebant Torchesiy et Harduic in Lincolia quintum denarium de geldo ciuitatis. Ad hanc quintam partem dabat Torchesyg. II. denarios. et Harduic tercium. De hac Torchesig habebat Morcar tercium denarium de omnibus consuetudinibus.

Before 1066 TORKSEY and Hardwick paid in Lincoln the fifth penny from the City's tax. Towards this fifth part Torksey paid two pennies and Hardwick the third. Morcar had the third penny of all the customary dues of this Torksey.

In Harduic habuerunt Suen et Godric. I. carucatam terrae. et ibi manebant. XII. homines. Modo habet Rogerius de busli medietatem. et episcopus Lincoliensis aliam medietatem. ad Sanctam MARIAM de Stou. Ibi cadit tercia pars geldi regis. quod rex non habet de illa quae pertinet ad Dorchesyg.

In Hardwick Swein and Godric had 1 carucate of land; 12 men lived there. Now Roger of Bully has half and the Bishop of Lincoln the other half at St Mary's of Stow. There falls to the third part of the King's tax which the King does not have from that which is due from TORKSEY.

Hic notantur qui habuerunt in Lincolescire sacam et socam. et Tol et Thiam. Episcopus Lincolie. Eddid regina. Abbas de Burg. Abbas de Ramesy. Abbas de Croiland. Haroldus comes. Morcar comes. Waltef comes. Radulfus comes. Ulf fenisc. Merlesuen. Turgot. Tochi filius. Outi. Stori. Radulfus stalre. Siuuard barn. Harold stalre. Fyach. Rolf. filius. Sceldeware. Godricus Filius. Toruort. . Achi. filius. Siuuardi et Wilac frater eius super terram patris eorum. Leuuine filius. Aluuine. Azer filius. Sualeuae. Ailric filius. marsete. Outi filius. azer. . Adestan filius. Godran. Tori filius. Rold. Toli filius. alsi. . Azer filius. Burgi. . Wluuard uuite. Ulf. Haminc. Bardi. Suan filius. Suaue.

Here are noted those who had full jurisdiction and market rights in Lincolnshire: The Bishop of Lincoln Queen Edith The Abbot of Peterborough The Abbot of Ramsey The Abbot of Crowland Earl Harold Earl Morcar Earl Waltheof Earl Ralph Ulf Fenman Merleswein Thorgot Lag Toki son of Auti Stori <of Bolingbroke> Ralph the constable Siward Barn Harold the constable Fiacc Rolf son of Skjaldvor Godric son of Thorfridh Aki son of Siward and Wiglac his brother over their father's land Leofwin son of Alwin A

Manerium In CHERCHEBI. habuit comes Morcar. V. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad IIII. carucas. Nunc habet Rex ibi. I. carucam. et XIIII. sochemannos cum II. carucis et. V. uillanos et V. bordarios. cum. I. caruca. Ibi dimidia aecclesia. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo VIII. libras cum pondere et arsione.

`ASWARDHURN' Wapentake In KIRKBY Earl Morcar had 5 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. Now the King has 1 plough there. 14 Freemen ploughs 5 villagers and 5 smallholders with 1 plough. 1/2 church. Value before 1066 £4; now £8 weighed and assayed. . King William holds it

Berewita Euedune iacet in supradicto Manerio. X bouatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Ibi. II. sochemanni et II. uillani habent. I. carucam. Ibi molendinum. V. solidorum et IIII. denariorum. et sedes. I. molendini. et VI. acrae prati.

EVEDON lies in the above manor. 10 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. 2 Freemen 2 villagers have 1 plough. A mill, 5s 4d; 1 mill site; meadow, 6 acres. . Earl Morcar had it . King William holds it

Ad eundem Manerium pertinet haec soca. Oustorp. I. cucarata. Welle. II. bouatae et dimidia. Hechintone. I. bouata. . Cornintone. I. bouata Simul ad geldum. I. carucata et dimidia. et dimidia bouata. Terra ad. I. carucam et dimidiam. In his habet rex. VII. sochemannos. habentes. I. carucam. Ibi. I. molendinum. XII. solidorum. et XIII. acrae prati. et XI. acrae siluae minute. et XX. acrae maresch.

Also to this manor belongs this jurisdiction: AUSTHORPE, 1 carucate; HOWELL, 2 1/2 bovates; HECKINGTON, 1 bovate; QUARRINGTON, 1 bovate. Altogether, 1 1/2 carucates and 1/2 bovate taxable. Land for 1 1/2 ploughs. In these the King has 7 Freemen who have 1 plough. 1 mill, 12s; meadow, 13 acres; underwood, 11 acres; marsh, 20 acres. . Earl Morcar had it . King William holds it

Manerium In CHIME. habuit Morcar comes. IIII. carucatas terrae et II. bouatas ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Nunc habet Rex ibi dimidiam carucam. et XII. uillanos et III. bordarios cum. II. carucis. Ibi. II. aecclesiae et I. presbyter. et II. acrae prati. et VI. piscine. IIII. or solidorum. Silua pastillis per loca. CC. et X. acrae. et septies. C. acrae maresc. T. R. E. ualuit XL. solidos. modo. LX. solidos cum pondere et arsione.

In KYME Earl Morcar had 4 carucates of land and 2 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. Now the King has 1/2 plough there. 12 villagers and 3 smallholders with 2 ploughs. 2 churches and 1 priest; meadow, 2 acres; 6 fishponds, 4s; pasturable woodland in places 210 acres; marsh, 700 acres. Value before 1066, 40s; now 60s weighed and assayed. . King William holds it

Manerium In BODEBI habuit Morcar comes. VIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. IX. carucas. Rex habet ibi. II. carucas. et XX. uillanos et II. bordarios. habentes. IIII. carucas. et XI. sochemanni de. III. carucatis. habentes. III. carucas. Ibi aecclesia et presbyter. una carucata terrae iacet in aecclesia. Ibi. I. molendinum III. solidorum. et CXX. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. libras. modo similiter XX. libras. cum pensione.

In BOOTHBY Earl Morcar had 8 carucates of land taxable. Land for 9 ploughs. The King has 2 ploughs there. 20 villagers and 2 smallholders who have 4 ploughs; 11 Freemen with 3 carucates who have 3 ploughs. A church and a priest; 1 carucate of land lies in the church . 1 mill, 3s; meadow, 120 acres. Value before 1066 £20; now the same, £20 weighed. . King William holds it

Manerium In WELLINGOURE. habuit Morcar. XVIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Rex habet nunc ibi. II. carucas. et VII. uillanos et VII. bordarios. cum. I. caruca. et XXVIII. sochemannos cum. VII. carucis. Ibi aecclesia et presbyter. habens. II. carucatas et II. bouatas de eadem terra. et Ipsa aecclesia pertinet ad aecclesiam Sacti Petri in Lincolia. Ibi. CXXIX. acrae prati. et aliae XIIII. acrae pertinent ad ecclesiam T. R. E. ualuit. XXX. libras. modo XV. libras cum pensione.

In WELLINGORE Morcar had 18 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. The King now has 2 ploughs there; 7 villagers and 7 smallholders with 1 plough; 28 Freemen with 7 ploughs. A church and a priest, who has 2 carucates and 2 bovates of this land. This church belongs to St Peter's Church in Lincoln. Meadow, 129 acres; another 14 acres belong to the Church. Value before 1066 £30; now £15 weighed. . King William holds it

Manerium In COLEBI. habuit Siuuard. VII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Rex habet nunc ibi. I. carucam. et V. uillanos et VI. bordarios cum. I. caruca. et X. sochemannos cum. II. carucas. et XXX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. Rex uero. Willelmus. apposuit hanc terram in Washingeburg. et ibi appreciatur. Presbyter et aecclesia. habens. I.

In COLEBY Siward had 7 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. The King now has 1 plough there. 5 villagers and 6 smallholders with 1 plough; 10 Freemen with 2 ploughs. Meadow, 30 acres. Value before 1066 £4; but King William placed this land in Washingborough; it is valued there. A priest and a church, which has 1 bovate of this land. . King William holds it

Soca et Berewita In ipsa Colebi sunt XII. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Ex his. est una caruca inland in Washingeburg. undecim uero sunt in soca. Hanc terram habuit Radulfus stalre. Nunc habet rex ibi XIIII. sochemannos et VII. uillanos cum. VX. VIII. carucis. et LX. acras prati. Huius precium. est in Washingeburg.

?S&B. Land for as many ploughs. 1 carucate of these is , in Washingborough, but 11 are in a jurisdiction. Ralph the constable had this land. Now the King has there 14 Freemen and 7 villagers with 8 ploughs; and meadow, 60 acres. Its valuation is in Washingborough. . King William holds it

Manerium In GRANTHAM. habuit Edid regina. XII. carucatas ad geldum. Non. est ibi terra arabilis extra uillam. Ibi non habuit quisque sacam et socam. praeter Elsuid monialem. quae dedit Sancto Petro de burg et hoc habet modo Colegrim. cum saca et soca. Regina Eddid habuit aulam. et II. carucatas. et terram de. III. carucis. sine geldo. Burgenses. C. et XI. Toftes sochemanniorum teignorum quater XX. III. minus. Nunc similiter habet rex. Iuo inuenit. I. carucam. et adhuc est. et LXXII. bordarios. AEcclesiam. I. cum. VIII. toftis. et IIII. molendina de XII. solidis. et VIII. acras prati sine geldo. De superioribus toftis. calumniatur episcopus dunelmensis. VII. toftas. quas habet Ernuinus presbyter. et hundret testimonium portat episcopo. T. R. E. tota Granham fuit soca ad. LII. libras. modo reddit. C. libras ad pondus. AEcclesia fuit tunc ad. VIII. libras. modo est ad. X. libras. sed non ualet nisi. C. solidos

In GRANTHAM Queen Edith had 12 carucates taxable. No arable land outside the town. No one had full jurisdiction except the nun Alswith, who gave it to St Peter's of Peterborough. Kolgrim has it now, with full jurisdiction. Queen Edith had a hall, 2 ploughs and land for 3 ploughs, without tax. 111 burgesses 80, less 3, plots of the Freemen of the thanes. Now the King has the same. Ivo found 1 plough - it is still there - and 72 smallholders and 1 church with 8 plots; 4 mills at 12s; meadow, 8 acres, without tax. Of the above plots the Bishop of Durham claims 7 plots, which Ernwin the priest has; the hundred bears witness for the Bishop. Before 1066 the whole of Grantham was a jurisdiction, at £52; it now pays £100 by weight. The church was then at £8; now it is at £10, but the value is only 100s. . King William holds it

Soca et Berewita In GUNFordebi. sunt VII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. IX. carucas. Tres carucatae sunt inland. et IIII. soca in Grandham. Ibi habet rex modo. I. carucam. et XXI. sochemannos. et I. uillanum et I. bordarium. habentes. VI. carucas. et C et V. acrae prati.

?S&B. Land for 9 ploughs. 3 carucates are and 4 a jurisdiction of Grantham. The King now has 1 plough. 21 Freemen, 1 villager and 1 smallholder who have 6 ploughs. Meadow, 105 acres. . King William holds it

Soca et Berewita In HERLAUESTUNE. sunt XII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. XVI. carucas. Nouem sunt in soca. et III. in aula Granham. Rex habet ibi nunc X. uillanos. et II. bordarios. cum. II. carucis. et LVIII. sochemannos. habentes. XIIII. carucas. Ibi. II. molendina. II. solidorum et LX. acrae prati. et LX. spineti.

?S&B. Land for 16 ploughs. 9 are in a jurisdiction and 3 in the hall of Grantham. The King now has 10 villagers and 2 smallholders with 2 ploughs; 58 Freemen who have 14 ploughs. 2 mills, 2s; meadow, 60 acres; spinney, 60 . . King William holds it

Soca In Sudstoches VI. et Nortstoches. III. sunt. IX. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. XII. carucas. Nunc habet rex ibi. I. carucam. et XVI. uillanos. cum. III. carucis. et X. sochemannos cum. II. carucis. et II. molendina reddentia XXI. solidos et IIII. denarios. Prati. IX. quarentenas. et III. lati. et XX. acras.

In `SOUTH STOKE' * 6 and `NORTH STOKE' 3 - 9 carucates of land taxable. Land for 12 ploughs. Now the King has 1 plough there. 16 villagers with 3 ploughs; 10 Freemen with 2 ploughs. 2 mills which pay 21s 4d; meadow, 9 furlongs long and 3 wide and 20 acres. . King William holds it

Soca In Nongtone. sunt. III. carucatae terre et V. bouatae |et dimidia ad geldum. Terra ad tantundem. Soca in Granham. Ibi habet rex. XIII. sochemannos. et VI. uillanos. cum. IIII. carucis. et I. molendinum XIII. solidorum et IIII denariorum. et III. acras prati.

In 3 carucates of land and 5 1/2 bovates taxable. Land for as much. A jurisdiction of Grantham. The King has 13 Freemen and 6 villagers with 4 ploughs. 1 mill, 13s 4d; meadow, 3 acres. . King William holds it

Manerium et Soca In magna PANPTUNE. habuit Edid regina. XII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. X. carucas. Nunc. est haec terra soca in Granham. Ibi sunt modo. X. uillani et III. bordarii. et XII. sochemanni. et dimidia caruca. Tantum inuenit Iuo ibi. Silua minuta. VIII. quarentenas longa. et III. lata.

?M&S. Land for 10 ploughs. This land is now a jurisdiction of Grantham. Now 10 villagers, 3 smallholders, 12 Freemen and 1/2 plough. Underwood 8 furlongs long and 3 wide. So much Ivo found there. . King William holds it

AD GRANHAM pertinet haec Soca. Sumerdebi II. carucatae et II. bouatae Sapretone V. carucatae et Breizbi. V. carucatae. Wellebi VIII. carucatae. Beltone V. carucatae. Herigerbi. IIII. carucatae. Dunetorp. II. carucatae. Lundetorp V. carucatae et VI. bouatae. Barchestone VIII. carucatae. Dentone. X. carucatae. Simul ad geldum. LV. carucatae. Terra ad. LX. carucas. Haec terra est tota in Asuuardetierne WAPENTAC.

To Grantham belongs this jurisdiction: SOMERBY, 2 carucates and 2 bovates; SAPPERTON, 5 carucates; BRACEBY, 5 carucates; WELBY, 8 carucates; BELTON, 5 carucates; HARROWBY, 4 carucates; `DUNSTHORPE', 2 carucates; LONDONTHORPE, 5 carucates and 6 bovates; BARKSTON, 8 carucates; DENTON, 10 carucates. Altogether 55 carucates taxable. Land for 60 ploughs. The whole of this land is in `Aswardhurn' Wapentake. . King William holds it
In Summerdebi. habet rex. VIII. sochemannos cum. II. carucis /et dimidia.
In SOMERBY the King has }. 8 Freemen with 2 1/2 ploughs. . King William holds it
In Sapretone et Brezbi. sunt XXIIII. sochemanni. et V. uillani et II. bordarii. cum. IX. carucis. et CXL. acrae prati. et XLVI. acrae silue pastillis. et LXIIII. acrae siluae minutae. et I. aecclesia
In SAPPERTON and BRACEBY there are 24 Freemen, 5 villagers and 2 smallholders with 9 ploughs. Meadow, 140 acres; pasturable woodland, 46 acres; underwood, 64 acres; 1 church. . King William holds it
In Wellebi. sunt XXXVII. sochemanni. et VII. uillani et IIII. bordarii. cum. X. carucis. et CLX. acrae prati. et CL. acrae siluae minutae.
In WELBY there are 37 Freemen, 7 villagers and 4 smallholders with 10 ploughs. Meadow, 160 acres; underwood, 150 acres; a church with a priest. . King William holds it
In Beltone. sunt XVIII. sochemanni. et XIIII. bordarii cum. IIII. carucis. et LXVIII. acrae prati.
In BELTON there are 18 Freemen and 14 smallholders with 4 ploughs. Meadow, 68 acres. . King William holds it
In Herigerbi. sunt XVI. sochemanni cum. IIII. carucis. et XXX. IIII. acrae prati.
In HARROWBY there are 16 Freemen with 4 ploughs. Meadow, 34 acres. . King William holds it
In Dunetorp. sunt. V. uillani et I. bordarius. et VIII. sochemanni. cum. II. carucis.
In `DUNSTHORPE' there are 5 villagers, 1 smallholder and 8 Freemen with 2 ploughs. Meadow, 20 acres. . King William holds it
In Lundertorp. sunt XXI. sochemanni. et VI. uillani cum. V. carucis. et XLIIII. acrae prati. et I. molendinum. X. solidorum.
In LONDONTHORPE there are 21 Freemen and 6 villagers with 5 ploughs. Meadow, 44 acres; 1 mill, 10s. . King William holds it
In Barchestone. sunt. XXX. V. sochemanni. et X. bordarii. cum. VI. carucis. et LXX. acrae prati. et II. molendina. quos habuit Turued. filius. Ulued.
In BARKSTON there are 35 Freemen and 10 smallholders with 6 ploughs. Meadow, 70 acres. 2 mills, which Thorfridh son of Wulffrith had. Their jurisdiction in Grantham. . King William holds it
In Dentone sunt |quater XX. ti acrae prati.
In DENTON there are meadow, 80 acres. . King William holds it

In Schillintune soca de granham. III. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Ibi. XIIII. uillani. et II. bordarii. et I. sochemannus. habent. III. carucas. et X. acras prati. et CXL. acras siluae minutae. Precium eius in Granham.

In SKILLINGTON, a jurisdiction of Grantham, 3 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. 14 villagers, 2 smallholders and 1 Freeman have 3 ploughs and meadow, 10 acres and underwood, 140 acres. Its valuation is in Grantham. . King William holds it

Manerium In BASINGEHAM. habuit Morcar comes. XX. IIII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad XVI. carucas. Ibi habet rex. II. carucas. et XXX. V. uillanos. et VIII. bordarios. et I sochemannms. habentes. VI. carucas. et II. molendina reddentia XXX. II. solidos. Ibi ecclesia et presbyter. et CCCC. et XX acrae prati. Totum XVI. quarentenas et dimidiam longum. et XV. quarentenas latum T. R. E. ualuit XXV. libras. modo XVI. libras

In BASSINGHAM Earl Morcar had 24 carucates of land taxable. Land for 16 ploughs. The King has 2 ploughs. 35 villagers, 8 smallholders and 1 Freeman who have 6 ploughs. 2 mills which pay 32s. A church and a priest; meadow, 420 acres. The whole 16 1/2 furlongs long and 15 furlongs wide. Value before 1066 £25; now £16. . King William holds it
In Torp. est soca huius Manerii. VI. bouatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. V. boues. Ibi. est. I. sochemannus cum dimidia caruca. et V. acrae prati.
In THORPE is a jurisdiction of this manor, 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 5 oxen. 1 Freeman with 1/2 plough. Meadow, 5 acres. . King William holds it

Manerium In TITE. habuit Algar comes. V. carucatas terre et I. bouatam ad geldum. Terra ad III. carucas. Ibi habet rex. I. carucam. et XVI. uillanos et V. bordarios. et I. sochemannum. habentes. VIII. carucas. et quater XX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. VIII. libras et I. piscaria cum silua reddebat. LXX. solidos. IIII. denariis minus. modo ualet totum. XV. libras.

In TYDD Earl Algar had 5 carucates of land and 1 bovate taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. The King has 1 plough. 16 villagers, 5 smallholders and 1 Freeman who have 8 ploughs. Meadow, 80 acres. Value before 1066 £8; 1 fishery with woodland paid 70s, less 4d. Value of the whole now £15. . King William holds it

Manerium In LUCTONE. habuit Algar comes. IIII. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad II. carucas. Ibi habet rex. VI. boues. et XVI. uillanos cum. IIII. carucis. et I. piscariam. XII. denariorum. et LX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. VIII. libras. modo similiter.

In LUTTON Earl Algar had 4 carucates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. The King has 6 oxen. 16 villagers with 4 ploughs. 1 fishery, 12d; meadow, 60 acres. Value before 1066 £8; now the same. . King William holds it
In Tite. est BereWita huius Manerii. II. carucatae et I. bouata terre ad geldum. Ibi sunt modo. VIII. uillani et I. sochemannus. habentes. I. carucam et II. os boues. et XXX. acrae prati.
In TYDD there is an outlier of this manor: 2 carucates and 1 bovate of land taxable. There are now 8 villagers and 1 Freeman who have 1 plough and 2 oxen. Meadow, 30 acres. . King William holds it

Manerium In GADENAI. habuit Algar comes. VIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad IIII. carucas. Ibi habet rex. VI. boues. et XVIII. uillanos cum. III. carucis. et XXX. acras prati. et piscariam. XII. denariorum. Longa. XX. quarentenas et lata XII. . . . T. R. E. ualuit. VIII. libras. modo. VI. libras.

In GEDNEY Earl Algar had 8 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. The King has 6 oxen. 18 villagers with 3 ploughs. Meadow, 30 acres; a fishery, 12d. 20 furlongs long and 12 wide Value before 1066 £8; now £6. . King William holds it

In Holebech. est soca huius Manerii. VIII. carucatae terrae et VI. bouatae ad geldum Ibi habet rex. XX. VI. sochemannos et V. bordarios cum XI. carucis. et quater XX. acras prati. Ista soca. est appreciata extra numerum supradictum. XVII. libras.

In HOLBEACH there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 8 carucates of land and 6 bovates taxable. The King has 26 Freemen and 5 smallholders with 11 ploughs; and meadow, 80 acres. This jurisdiction is valued at £17 in addition to the above sum. . King William holds it
In eadem holebech et Copelade sunt. V. carucatae terrae ad geldum. quas comes alanus tenebat. modo sunt in manu regis.
In HOLBEACH also and WHAPLODE there are 5 carucates of land taxable, which Count Alan held; now they are in the King's hands. . King William holds it

In FLeot habuit Algar comes. VI. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. IIII. carucas. Ibi habet rex. VI. boues et VIII. uillanos. cum. II. carucis et dimidia. et I. piscariam. XVI. denariorum. et II. salinas. II. solidorum. et quingentae acrae prati. Una leua longum. et I. latum. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo. L. solidos

In FLEET Earl Algar had 6 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. The King has 6 oxen and 8 villagers with 2 1/2 ploughs; and 1 fishery, 16d; 2 salt-houses, 2s; meadow, 500 acres. 1 league long and 1 wide. Value before 1066 £4; now 50s. . King William holds it

Manerium In NETELHAM. habuit Edid regina XII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad XVI. carucas. Ibi habet rex. III. carucas. et XXVIII. uillanos. et XII. bordarios. et I. sochemannum. habentes. XI. carucas. Totum XX. quarentenas longum. et XX. latum. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. IIII. libras. modo. XXX. libras.

In NETTLEHAM Queen Edith had 12 carucates of land taxable. Land for 16 ploughs. The King has 3 ploughs. 28 villagers, 12 smallholders and 1 Freeman who have 11 ploughs. The whole 20 furlongs long and 20 wide. Value before 1066 £24; now £30. . King William holds it

In Duneham. est soca huius Manerii. VIII. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Ibi sunt. XVIII. sochemanni cum. VI. carucis. et L. acrae prati. De hac terra habet Odo balistarius. II. carucatas et dimidiam.

In DUNHOLME there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 8 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. 18 Freemen with 6 ploughs. Meadow, 50 acres. Of this land Odo the bowman has 2 1/2 carucates. . King William holds it
In Sonetorp VI. bouatae et Wichingebi. I. carucata carucata. Soca in Netelham. In Reresbi II. bouatae. soca ibidem.
In SWINTHORPE, 6 bovates, and WICKENBY, 1 carucate. A jurisdiction of Nettleham. In REASBY, 2 bovates. A jurisdiction also there. . King William holds it

Manerium In CHIRCHETONE. habuit Eduinus comes. VIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. XVI. carucas. Ibi habet rex modo. IIII. carucas in dominio. et quater XX. uillanos. et XXX. VII. bordarios. cum XVIII. carucis. et I. molendinum. XII. denariorum. et CC. acras prati. Duas leuas longa. et XX. quarentenas lata. T. R. E. ualuit XX. IIII. libras. modo quater XX. libras.

In KIRTON Earl Edwin had 8 carucates of land taxable. Land for 16 ploughs. Now the King has 4 ploughs in lordship. 80 villagers and 37 smallholders with 18 ploughs. 1 mill, 12d; meadow, 200 acres. 2 leagues long and 20 furlongs wide. Value before 1066 £24; now £80. . King William holds it

Ad ipsum Manerium pertinet soca haec. Glenteuurde VI cucatae et II. bouatae et dimidia. HelmesIIII. carucatae et II. bouatae welle. Herpeswelle II. carucatae et VI. bouatae et dimidia. Esnetrebi IIII. bouatae. Sassebi. I. carucata. Grangeham. IIII. carucatae Coringeham. I. carucata. Asebi. I. carucata. Iopeham II. carucatae et dimidia. Springetorp et VI. carucataecoringeham. IX. uem bouatae Mortum Sumerdebi II. carucatae. Blitone III. carucatae. . Pileham. I. carucata. Torp. II. carucatae. Aschebi I. carucata. Hiboldestone. II. carucatae et dimidia. Staintone VI. bouatae. Reburne IX. bouatae. Brunebi. V. carucatae. Aschebi XIII. bouatae. Budlesforde. II. carucatae. Iadulfestorp I. cucata. Witrintone. IIII. carucatae. . Escumetorp III. carucatae et VI. bouatae et II. partes. I. bouatae. Simul ad geldum L. VIIII. carucatae terre. Terra. est ad. LX. VIIII. carucas.

To this manor belongs this jurisdiction: GLENTWORTH, 6 carucates and 2 1/2 bovates; HEMSWELL, 4 carucates and 2 bovates; HARPSWELL, 2 carucates and 6 1/2 bovates; SNITTERBY, 4 bovates; SAXBY, 1 carucate; GRAYINGHAM, 4 carucates; CORRINGHAM, 1 carucate; AISBY, 1 carucate; HEAPHAM, 2 1/2 carucates; SPRINGTHORPE and CORRINGHAM, 6 carucates; MORTON, 9 bovates; SOMERBY, 2 carucates; BLYTON, 3 carucates; PILHAM, 1 carucate; [NORTHORPE, 2 carucates; ASHBY, 1 carucate; HIBALDSTOW, 2 1/2 carucates; `STAINTON', 6 bovates; REDBOURNE, 9 bovates; BRUMBY, 5 carucates; ASHBY, 13 bovates; BOTTESFORD, 2 carucates; YADDLETHORPE, 1 carucate; WINTERTON, 4 carucates; SCUNTHORPE, 3 carucates, 6 bovates and 2 parts of 1 bovate. Altogether 59 carucates of land taxable. Land for 69 ploughs. . Earl Edwin had it . King William holds it
. In Glenteuurde. sunt XX. IIII. sochemanni et VI. bordarii. cum. V. carucis. et CC. et XII. acrae prati.
In GLENTWORTH there are 24 Freemen and 6 smallholders with 5 ploughs. Meadow, 212 acres. . Earl Edwin had it . King William holds it
In Helmeswelle. sunt XVII. sochemanni. et I. uillanus et VII. bordarii cum. III. carucis. et CLXXIII. acrae prati.

In THEALBY, DARBY and BURTON the King has 6 plots and 1/2 market which belong to Kirton. HIBALDSTOW is an outlier not a jurisdiction; in GRAYINGHAM there are 2 carucates ; in SPRINGTHORPE 1/2 carucate is . All the rest is a jurisdiction. . King William holds it

?M&B. Land for 6 ploughs. The King has 1 plough in lordship; 40 villagers and 12 Freemen with 3 ploughs. A church and a priest, which the Bishop of Lincoln claims. 4 mills, 13s 4d; meadow, 60 acres. Value before 1066 £30; now £50. . King William holds it

Ad huius Manerii aulam pertinent Catenai et Usun. IIII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VIII. carucas. Ibi. in dominio. II. carucae. et XX uillani et XV. sochemanni. et X. bordarii. habentes. IX. carucas. Ibi CCC. et LX.

. II. Maneria In ULINGEHAM. habuit Elmaer. III. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra. est ad. IIII. carucas. Nunc habet Thomas archiepiscopus. et Willelmus de eo. In dominio. In WILLINGHAM Almer had 3 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. Now Archbishop Thomas has it, and William from him. In lordship 2 1/2 ploughs; 11 villagers and 2 smallholders who have 2 ploughs and meadow, 77 acres. Value before 1066 £4 10s; now the same.
In Barcuurde. est soca huius Manerii. VI. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam et dimidiam. Ibi. VII. sochemanni. et I. bordarius. habent. I. carucam. Ibi XIII. acrae prati

In BARKWITH there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 1/2 ploughs. 7 Freemen and 1 smallholder have 1 plough. Meadow, 13 acres. Willingham. Almer had it. William has it

Manerium In PANTONE. habuit Aluric. II. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad III. carucas. Nunc habet Gislebertus homo archiepiscopi in dominio. I. carucam et dimidiam. et I. uillanum. et XII. sochemannos. cum. I. caruca et dimidia. et XVII. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit XXX. solidos. modo. XL. solidos.

In PANTON Aelfric had 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. Now Gilbert, the Archbishop's man, has in lordship 1 1/2 ploughs; 1 villager and 12 Freemen with 1 1/2 ploughs. Meadow, 17 acres. Value before 1066, 30s; now 40s.
In Barcuurde. est soca huius Manerii. VI. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Wasta. est. Ibi. XV. acrae prati.

In BARKWITH there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. It is waste. Meadow, 15 acres. [Jurisdiction of Panton. Aelfric had it. Gilbert, the Archbishop's man, has it

Manerium In GRISEBI. habuit AElmar. II. carucatas terre et II. bouatas ad geldum. Terra ad. IIII. carucas et dimidiam. . II. Maneria In STALINGEBURG. habuit Elaf. I. carucatam terre et II. bouatas ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas et dimidiam. Ibi Herbertus homo archiepiscopi habet in dominio. I. carucam. et V. uillanos et III. sochemannos. et I. bordarium. cum una caruca. Ibi molendinum dimidium. XXX. II. denariorum. et II. salinae. et quater XX. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit XXX. solidos. modo. L. solidos.

In STALLINGBOROUGH Elaf had 1 carucate of land and 2 bovates taxable. Land for 2 1/2 ploughs. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has in lordship 1 plough; 5 villagers, 3 Freemen and 1 smallholder with 1 plough. 1/2 mill, 32d; 2 salt-houses; meadow, 80 acres. Value before 1066, 30s; now 50s.

SOCA Huius Manerii. Heghelinge. I. bouata. Cleia III. bouatae. Ternescou II. bouatae. Idest VI. bouatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam et II. boues. Ibi. V. sochemanni et III. uillani habent. I. carucam. et XX. V. acra prati.

Jurisdiction of this manor: HEALING, 1 bovate; CLEE, 3 bovates; `THRUNSCOE', 2 bovates. That is, 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough and 2 oxen. 5 Freemen and 3 villagers have 1 plough. Meadow, 25 acres. [Jurisdiction of Stallingborough. Elaf had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it

Ibi Willelmus homo archiepiscopi habet dimidiam carucam. et II. sochemannos et II. bordarios. et dimidium molendinum. III. solidorum. et IIII. denariorum. T. R. E. ualuit. X. solidos. modo similiter.

In KEELBY Elaf had 4 1/2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough and 1 ox. William , the Archbishop's man, has 1/2 plough. 2 Freemen and 2 smallholders. 1/2 mill, 3s 4d. Value before 1066, 10s; now the same.

Manerium In CUCUALT. habuit. Fulchri. VII. bouatas |et. III. partes. I. bouataeterre ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam et VI. boues. Ibi Willelmus homo archiepsicopi habet. I. carucam. et I. uillanum et I. bordarium. et I. sochemannum. Ipsi. I. caruca. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. solidos. modo similiter.

In CUXWOLD Fulcric had 7 bovates of land and 3 parts of 1 bovate taxable. Land for 1 plough and 6 oxen. William , the Archbishop's man, has 1 plough. 1 villager, 1 smallholder and 1 Freeman; they 1 plough. Value before 1066, 20s; now the same.
In Sualun. est soca |et inland huius Manerii. I. carucata terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi sunt. III. sochemanni et I. bordarius. cum dimidia caruca.

In SWALLOW there is a jurisdiction and of this manor; 1 carucate of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. 3 Freemen and 1 smallholder with 1/2 plough. [Jurisdiction and of Cuxwold. Fulcric had it. William , the Archbishop's man, has it

Manerium In LESSINTONE. habuit Lanbecarle. IIII. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. X. carucas. Ibi Herbertus homo archiepiscopi habet in dominio. II. carucas. et XVI. uillanos et VIII. bordarios. et IIII. sochemannos. habentes. V. carucas. Ibi quater XX. acrae prati. et quater XX. acrae siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo. VII. libras et X. solidos. Tailla. XL. solidos.

In LISSINGTON Lambakarl had 4 carucates of land taxable. Land for 10 ploughs. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has in lordship 2 ploughs; 16 villagers, 8 smallholders and 4 Freemen who have 5 ploughs. Meadow, 80 acres; underwood, 80 acres. Value before 1066 £4; now £7 10s. Exactions 40s.
In Sonetorp. I. carucata terre et dimidia geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi in dominio. I. caruca. et II. uillani. et XII. acrae et dimidia prati.

and jurisdiction of this manor: In SWINTHORPE 1 1/2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. In lordship 1 plough; 2 villagers. Meadow, 12 1/2 acres. [Jurisdiction and of Lissington. Lambakarl had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it
In Esneleunt. II. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad dimidiam carucam. Wasta. est. /Ibi. III. acrae prati.
In SNELLAND 2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1/2 plough. It is waste. Meadow, 3 acres. [Jurisdiction and of Lissington. Lambakarl had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it
In Houtune. I. carucata terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi. V. sochemanni et V. uillani habent. III. carucas. et XIII. acrae prati.

In HOLTON 1 carucate of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. 5 Freemen and 5 villagers have 3 ploughs. Meadow, 13 acres. [Jurisdiction and of Lissington. Lambakarl had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it
Soca In Bechelinge. II. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad dimidiam carucam. Ibi. IIII. sochemanni habent. I. carucam et VI. boues arantes.
In BECKERING 2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1/2 plough. 4 Freemen have 1 plough and 6 oxen ploughing. [Jurisdiction and of Lissington. Lambakarl had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it

f Manerium In BENINGURDE. habuit Goduinus. II. carucatas terre et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas et dimidiam. Nunc Osbernus presbyter habet de archiepiscopo. In dominio. II. carucas. et VII. uillani. et II. bordarii. et VI. sochemanni. habentes. I. carucam et dimidiam. Ibi aecclesia. et sedes. I. molendini. et LX. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit XXX. solidos. modo. LXX. solidos.

In BENNIWORTH Godwin had 2 1/2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 1/2 ploughs. Now Osbern the priest has it from the Archbishop. In lordship 2 ploughs; 7 villagers, 2 smallholders and 6 Freemen who have 1 1/2 ploughs. A church; 1 mill site; meadow, 60 acres. Value before 1066, 30s; now 70s.

Manerium In AGETORNE. habuit Aluuinus. I. carucatam et V. bouatas terre ad geldum. Manerium et Auti. III. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas et dimidiam. Ibi Willelmus homo archiepiscopi habet. I. carucam. et VI. uillani et VII. bordarii. et II. sochemanni habent. III. carucas. et V. boues. Ibi dimidia aecclesia et II. molendina. IIII. solidorum. et LX. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit LX. solidos. modo. XL. solidos. In Soca huius Manerii habet archiepiscopus. Thomas. III. bouatas ad geldum.

In HACKTHORN Alwin had 1 carucate and 5 bovates of land taxable, and Auti 3 bovates of land taxable. Land for 3 1/2 ploughs. William , the Archbishop's man, has 1 plough. 6 villagers, 7 smallholders and 2 Freemen have 3 ploughs and 5 oxen. 1/2 church; 2 mills, 4s; meadow, 60 acres. Value before 1066, 60s; now 40s. In the jurisdiction of this manor Archbishop Thomas has 3 bovates taxable.

Manerium In RIGESBI. habuit Aldene. VI. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra Terra ad. I. carucam et II. boues. Ibi Herbertus homo archiepiscopi habet. I. carucam. et III. uillanos et IIII. bordarios. cum. II. bobus. Ibi aecclesia et presbyter. et quater XX et X. acrae silue pastilis. et LX. acrae siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit. LX. solidos. modo similiter.

In RIGSBY Healfdene had 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough and 2 oxen. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has 1 plough. 3 villagers and 4 smallholders with 2 oxen. A church and a priest; pasturable woodland, 90 acres; underwood, 60 acres. Value before 1066, 60s; now the same.
In Sudtune et Dreuistor|p et Herdetorp. est inland huius Manerii. dimidia carucata terre ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Ibi. VI. uillani habent dimidiam carucam. et XL. acrae prati.

In SUTTON, TRUSTHORPE and ADDLETHORPE there is of this manor; 1/2 carucate of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. 6 villagers have 1/2 plough. Meadow, 40 acres. had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it

SOCA eiusdem Manerii. Righesbi IIII. bouatae. Halebi III. bouatae. Tatebi I. carucata. hoc Manerium fuit. Idest ad geldum. XV. bouatae terre. Terra ad. III. carucas et dimidiam. In eis. VII. sochemanni et II. uillani et I. bordarius. habent. I. carucam et dimidiam. et XX. acras prati. et XII. acrae siluae. et XX. acrae minutae. haec SOCA T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo XL. solidos

Jurisdiction also of this manor: RIGSBY, 4 bovates; AILBY, 3 bovates; , 1 carucate: this was a manor. That is 15 bovates of land taxable. Land for 3 1/2 ploughs. In them 7 Freemen, 2 villagers and 1 smallholder have 1 1/2 ploughs and meadow, 20 acres; woodland, 12 acres; under, 20 acres. Value of this jurisdiction before 1066 £4; now 40s. had it. Herbert , the Archbishop's man, has it

. II. Maneria In TESFORDE. habuerunt Elmer et Arnui. III. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. IIII. carucas. Ibi Gislebertus homo archiepiscopi habet. II. carucas. et I. bouem. et VIII. uillanos. et VII. sochemannos cum. II. carucis. Ibi aecclesia. et I. molendinum. IIII. solidorum. et CXX. acraes prati. et VI. acrae siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit XXX. solidos. modo. IIII. libras.

In TETFORD Almer and Ernwin had 3 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. Gilbert, the Archbishop's man, has 2 ploughs and 1 ox. 8 villagers and 7 Freemen with 2 ploughs. A church; 1 mill, 4s; meadow, 120 acres; underwood, 6 acres. Value before 1066, 30s; now £4.
In Ormesbi. est soca huius Manerii dimidia carucata terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. boues. Ibi. I. sochemannus et I. uillanus cum dimidia caruca. et XX. acrae prati.

In ORMSBY there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 1/2 carucate of land taxable. Land for 6 oxen. 1 Freeman and 1 villager with 1/2 plough. Meadow, 20 acres. had it. Gilbert, the Archbishop's man, has it

In Haintone. est Soca de Torp. I. carucata terre et dimidia ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Ibi. III. sochemanni et III. bordarii habent. V. boues. arantes. et quater. XX. acrae prati. Elmer habuit. Willelmus habet de ARCHIEPISCOPO.

In HAINTON there is a jurisdiction of ; 1 1/2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. 3 Freemen and 3 smallholders have 5 oxen ploughing. Meadow, 80 acres. Almer had it; William has it from the Archbishop.
In SOCA de Linberge habet archiepiscopus. III. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam.
In the jurisdiction of Limber the Archbishop has 3 bovates _ of land taxable. Land for 1 plough.
In Stalinburg. est inland de Linberge. dimidia bouata terre ad geldum. Terra ad. I. bouem. Ibi archiepiscopus. I. uillanum cum. I. boue arans. et sedem molendini. et dimidiam laninam.
In STALLINGBOROUGH there is of Limber; 1/2 bovate of land taxable. Land for 1 ox. The Archbishop 1 villager with 1 ox ploughing; and a mill site; 1/2 wool-house.
In Neutone habet archiepiscopus. C. acras prati pertinentes ad Lanun.
In NEWTON the Archbishop has 100 acres of meadow which belong to Laneham.

Manerium In HERPESWELLE. habuit Aluuin. V. bouatas terre et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Ibi Willelmus homo archiepiscopi habet. I. uillanum et V. sochemannos. cum. VI. bobus arantes. et XXXIX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. XVI. solidos. modo. X. solidos.

In HARPSWELL Alwin had 5 1/2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. William , the Archbishop's man, has 1 villager and 5 Freemen with 6 oxen ploughing; and meadow, 39 acres. Value before 1066, 16s; now 10s.

Manerium In WLURICESBI. habuit Aluric. II. carucatas terre et II. bouatas. ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Nunc archiepiscopi habet ibi. I. carucam. et I. uillanum. et X. sochemannos cum. I. caruca. et XX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit III. libras. modo. XL. solidos.

In WORLABY Aelfric had 2 carucates of land and 2 bovates taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. Now the Archbishop has 1 plough there. 1 villager and 10 Freemen with 1 plough. Meadow, 20 acres. Value before 1066 £3; now 40s.

Manerium In DUSEBI. habuit Aldene. III. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Nunc hugo homo archiepiscopi habet ibi. I. carucam. et IX. uillanos et III. bordarios. et I. sochemannum. habentes. II. carucas. et I. molendinum. III. solidorum. et XXX. II. acrae prati. et XX. acrae silue minutae. T. R. E. ualuit III. libras. modo. IIII. libras. Tailla. XX. solidos.

In DOWSBY Healfdene had 3 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. Now Hugh, the Archbishop's man, has 1 plough there. 9 villagers, 3 smallholders and 1 Freeman who have 2 ploughs. 1 mill, 3s; meadow, 32 acres; underwood, 20 acres. Value before 1066 £3; now £4. Exactions 20s.
In Bichere. est inland huius Manerii. I. bouata terre et dimidia ad geldum. Terra ad tantundem. Wasta. est. Ibi. est. I. salina. Wasta.
In BICKER there is of this manor; 1 1/2 bovates of land taxable. Land for as much. It is waste. 1 salt-house; waste. had it. Hugh, the Archbishop's man, has it
In GREIBI. est SOCA eiusdem Manerii. I. carucata terre. Terra ad. I. carucam. Ibi. VI. sochemanni habent. I. carucam et I. bouem.
In GRABY there is a jurisdiction also of this manor; 1 carucate of land. Land for 1 plough. 6 Freemen have 1 plough and 1 ox. had it. Hugh, the Archbishop's man, has it

Manerium In BILLINGEBURG. habuit Turchil. V. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. V. boues. Nunc Walterius de Aincurt de archiepiscopo. habet. Ibi dimidia caruca. et I. uillanus et I. bordarius. et VII. acras prati. et I. sedem molendini. Ualet. X. solidos.

In BILLINGBOROUGH Thorkil had 5 bovates of land taxable. Land for 5 oxen. Now Walter of Aincourt has them from the Archbishop. 1/2 plough. 1 villager and 1 smallholder. Meadow, 7 acres; 1 mill site. Value 10s.

Manerium In HORBELINGE. habuit Turchil. IIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Nunc Walterius de archiepiscopo habet. In dominio. I. caruca. et IX. uillani et I. bordarius. et VIII. sochemanni habentes III. carucas. Ibi aecclesia. et XX. acrae prati. Terra arabilis. I. leuam longa. et I. lata. T. R. E. ualuit. VI. libras. modo XL. solidos. Tailla. XX. solidos.

In HORBLING Thorkil had 4 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. Now Walter has them from the Archbishop. In lordship 1 plough; 9 villagers, 1 smallholder and 8 Freemen who have 3 ploughs. A church; meadow, 20 acres; arable land 1 league long and 1 wide. Value before 1066 £6; now 40s. Exactions 20s.

Manerium In WIME. habuit Baco. I. carucatam terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Manerium et Uluuine. I. carucatam ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Nunc Walchelinus homo archiepiscopo habet ibi. II. carucas. et IIII. uillanos et II. bordarios cum. I. caruca. Ibi aecclesia. de qua Eduuard cilt habet terciam partem. et I. sedem molendini. et XXVII. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit XL. solidos. modo. L. solidos. Tailla. XI. solidos.

In WITHAM Baca had 1 carucate of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. And Wulfwin 1 carucate taxable. Land for 1 plough. Now Walkelin , the Archbishop's man, has 2 ploughs there. 4 villagers and 2 smallholders with 1 plough. A church of which Edward the noble has the third part; 1 mill site; meadow, 27 acres. Value before 1066, 40s; now 50s. Exactions 11s.
In Tuiforde. est soca de Nortuuine. dimidia carucata terrae ad geldum. Terra ad dimidiam carucam. Ibi. II. bordarii cum. II. bobus arantes. Ibi. III. acrae prati. et XXVII. acrae siluae minutae.

In `TWYFORD' there is a jurisdiction of North Witham; 1/2 carucate of land taxable. Land for 1/2 plough. 2 smallholders who plough with 2 oxen. Meadow, 3 acres; underwood, 27 acres. , the Archbishop's man, has it

Manerium In WIME. habuit Aluuinus. II. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi Walchelin homo archiepiscopi. II. carucas. et VI. uillanos et II. sochemannos. et I. censorem. Ibi quater XX. acrae prati. et IX. et CXX. acrae siluae in Warnode drogonis. et XL. acre siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit XX. solidos. modo XX. V. solidos. Tailla. V. solidos.

In WITHAM Alwin had 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. Walkelin , the Archbishop's man, 2 ploughs and 6 villagers, 2 Freemen and 1 tributary. Meadow, 89 acres; woodland in Drogo , 120 acres; underwood, 40 acres. Value before 1066, 20s; now 25s. Exactions 5s.

In SCHILLINTUNE. habuit Morcar comes. III. carucatas terre ad geldum. et Friguist et Bridmer. I. carucatam terre ad geldum Terra ad. V. carucas et dimidiam. Frigesoca in Schillintune. Ibi Walchelinus homo archiepiscopi. II. carucas. et XIII. uillani et V. sochemanni. habentes. IIII. carucas. Ibi CXXI. acrae et dimidia prati. et LX. acrae silue pastilis in Warnode episcopi. Remigii. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo similiter. Tailla. XX. solidos.

In SKILLINGTON Earl Morcar had 3 carucates of land taxable and Fredegaest and Brictmer 1 carucate of land taxable. Land for 5 1/2 ploughs. A free jurisdiction of Skillington. Walkelin , the Archbishop's man, 2 ploughs. 13 villagers and 5 Freemen who have 4 ploughs. Meadow, 121 1/2 acres; pasturable woodland in Bishop Remigius' , 60 acres. Value before 1066 £4; now the same. Exactions 20s.

Manerium Ibidem in ESTONE. habuit Siuuard. III. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas et VI. bouatas. Ibi Osbernus homo archiepiscopi habet. I. carucam. et XII. uillanos et VI. bordarios. Ibi dimidia aecclesia. et I. molendinum. VIII. solidorum. et X. prati. et CC. acrae siluae pastilis. et CCXL. acrae siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit XL. solidos. modo XL. solidos.

There also in EASTON Siward had 3 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs and 6 oxen. Osbern, the Archbishop's man, has 1 plough. 12 villagers and 6 smallholders. 1/2 church; 1 mill, 8s; meadow, 10 acres; pasturable woodland, 200 acres; underwood, 240 acres. Value before 1066, 40s; now 40s.

Soca In COLSTEWRDE habuit Morcar comes. III. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Soca in Schellintune. Ibi. IIII. sochemanni et IIII. uillani habent. II. carucas. et CXX. acrae siluae pastilis per loca.
In COLSTERWORTH Earl Morcar had 3 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. A jurisdiction of Skillington. 4 Freemen and 4 villagers have 2 ploughs. Pasturable woodland in places, 120 acres.

Manerium In BELINGEI. habuit Suuen. XII. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. IIII. carucas. Ibi Walchelinus homo archiepiscopi et II. filii Suuen. II. carucas. et III. uillanos. et XV. sochemannos habentes. IIII. carucas. Ibi XVI. acrae prati. et III. sedes piscariarum. T. R. E. ualuit IIII. libras. modo similiter. et V. solidos plus.

In BILLINGHAY Swein had 12 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. Walkelin , the Archbishop's man, and the two sons of Swein 2 ploughs. 3 villagers and 15 Freemen who have 4 ploughs. Meadow, 16 acres; 3 sites for fisheries. Value before 1066 £4; now the same and 5s more.

In Walecote. est inland huius Manerii. VIII. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. carucas. Ibi. XV. sochemanni et I. uillanus habent. VI. carucas et IIII. acras prati. et XLVI. acrae siluae minutae.

In WALCOT there is of this manor; 8 carucates of land taxable. Land for 6 ploughs. 15 Freemen and 1 villager have 6 ploughs and meadow, 4 acres; underwood, 46 acres. [ of Billinghay. Swein had it. Walkelin , the Archbishop's man, and the two sons of Swein have it

Manerium In LAUINTONE. habuit Ulf. IIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. V. carucas. Ibi Rannulfus clericus archiepiscopi. II. carucas habet in dominio. et XII. uillanos et I. sochemannum et IIII. bordarios habentes. VI. carucas. Ibi XXX. VI. acrae prati. et CX. acrae siluae minutae. In Warnode Widonis de credun. Terra arabilis et pascuae. II. leuas longa. et VI. quarentenas lata. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo. LX. solidos.

In LENTON Ulf had 4 carucates of land taxable. Land for 5 ploughs. Ranulf, a cleric of the Archbishop's, has 2 ploughs in lordship; 12 villagers, 1 Freeman and 4 smallholders who have 6 ploughs. Meadow, 36 acres; underwood, 110 acres. In the of Guy of Craon. Arable land and pastureland 2 leagues long and 6 furlongs wide. Value before 1066 £4; now 60s. Land of the Bishop of Durham

. III. Manerium In BROTULBI. habuit Stepiot. V. bouattas terrae ad geldum. Soca ibidem. Terra ad. VI. boues. Nunc episcopus dunelmensis. et colsuuen de eo. habet hanc terram et colit eam. T. R. E. III. solidos. modo. IIII. solidos.

In BRATTLEBY Stiupi had 5 bovates of land taxable. A jurisdiction also there. Land for 6 oxen. Now the Bishop of Durham, and Kolsveinn from him, has this land and cultivates it. before 1066, 3s; now 4s.

Manerium In Snardesforde. habuit Siuuard. II. carucatas terrae et II. bouatas ad geldun. Terra ad. II. carucas et VI. boues. Nunc episcopus dunelmensis et Colsuan de eo habet. I. carucam. et XIIII. sochemannos cum. II. carucis. et LX. acras prati. T. R. E. XX. solidos. modo. XXX. solidos. et tailla. X. solidos.

In SNARFORD Siward had 2 carucates of land and 2 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs and 6 oxen. Now the Bishop of Durham, and Kolsveinn from him, has 1 plough. 14 Freemen with 2 ploughs. Meadow, 60 acres. before 1066, 20s; now 30s. Exactions 10s.

Manerium In BERLINGE. habuit Dane. IX. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad I. carucam et dimidiam. Nunc Colsuan de episcopo. In dominio. I. caruca. et II. uillani et I. bordarius cum. I. caruca. et XX. acrae prati. et XX. acrae siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit XVI. solidos. modo. XX. solidos.

In BARLINGS Dene had 9 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 1/2 ploughs. Now Kolsveinn from the Bishop. In lordship 1 plough; 2 villagers and 1 smallholder with 1 plough. Meadow, 20 acres; underwood, 20 acres. Value before 1066, 16s; now 20s.

Manerium In BLIBURG. habuit Redulf. VII. bouatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Nunc monachi dunelmi habent ibi. II. carucas. et III. uillanos. et I. bordarium. et I. sochemannum cum. I. caruca. et XX. acras prati. et dimidiam aecclesiam inter episcopum et Gozelinum filium. Lanberti. T. R. E. ualuit XX. solidos. modo XXX. solidos.

In BLYBOROUGH Redulph had 7 bovates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. Now the monks of Durham have 2 ploughs there and 3 villagers, 1 smallholder and 1 Freeman with 1 plough; and meadow, 20 acres; 1/2 church between the Bishop and Jocelyn son of Lambert. Value before 1066, 20s; now 30s.

Manerium In BRACHELESBI. habuit Goduin. I. carucatam terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi Nigel homo episcopi habet. III. uillanos cum. I. boue. et XXX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. XL. solidos. modo X. solidos.
NORTH RIDING In BROCKLESBY Godwin had 1 carucate of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. Nigel, the Bishop's man, has 3 villagers with 1 ox and meadow, 30 acres. Value before 1066, 40s; now 10s.

. II. Maneria In FUGELESTOU. habuerunt Aldene et Elmar. I. carucatam terrae et II. bouatas ad geldu. Terra ad. II. carucas et dimidiam. Ibi Walbertus homo episcopi habet XVI. uillanos et X. bordarios cum dimidia caruca. et I. carucam in dominio et XI. salinas. II. solidorum. et LX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. LX. solidos. modo. IIII. libras.

In FULSTOW Healfdene and Almer had 1 carucate of land and 2 bovates taxable. Land for 2 1/2 ploughs. Walbert <of Fulstow>, the Bishop's man, has 16 villagers and 10 smallholders with 1/2 plough. 1 plough in lordship. 11 salt-houses, 2s; meadow, 60 acres. Value before 1066, 60s; now £4.

. II. Maneria In NEUTONE habuit Grinchel. XI. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi Walbertus homo episcopi habet. I. carucam. et II. uillanos et II. bordarios cum dimidia caruca. Ibi aecclesia et XL. acras prati. et In Turgrimbi. I. molendinum. . et X. acrae prati. quod Norman de Areci tenet iniuste. T. R. E. ualuit. C. solidos. modo XXX. solidos. Ibi halla cum /tofta et soca et saca.

In NEWTON Grimkel had 11 bovates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. Walbert <of Fulstow>, the Bishop's man, has 1 plough. 2 villagers and 2 smallholders with 1/2 plough. A church; meadow, 40 acres. In Thorganby 1 mill which Norman of Arcy holds wrongfully; meadow, 10 acres. Value before 1066, 100s; now 30s. A hall with a plot and full jurisdiction.

Manerium In BOLINTONE. habuit Aluric. III. bouatas terrae et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Ibi Nigel homo episcopi habet dimidiam carucam. et VI. uillanos cum. I. caruca. et X. acras prati. et CLX. acrae siluae minutae. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. solidos. modo X. solidos.

SOUTH RIDING In BULLINGTON Aelfric had 3 1/2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. Nigel, the Bishop's man, has 1/2 plough. 6 villagers with 1 plough. Meadow, 10 acres; underwood, 160 acres. Value before 1066, 20s; now 10s.

Manerium In HARDUUIC. habuit Aluric. II. bouatas terre et terciam partem unius bouatae ad geldum. Terra ad. V. boues. Nigel habet de episcopo et Wasta. est. Ibi XXVII. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. X. solidos. modo III. solidos.
In `HARDWICK' Aelfric had 2 bovates of land and the third part of 1 bovate taxable. Land for 5 oxen. Nigel has it from the Bishop. It is waste. Meadow, 27 acres. Value before 1066, 10s; now 3s.
In Langetona. sunt. III. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad VI. boues Soca. est in Stratone. et LXV. acrae siluae minutae ibi.
In LANGTON 3 bovates of land taxable. Land for 6 oxen. It is a jurisdiction of ` Sturton'. Underwood, 65 acres there.
Soca et Berewita In Wispinctune. II. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. IIII. or carucas. Inland et Soca. in Stratone et Cherchebi. Ibi IX. sochemanni et VI. bordarii. cum. III. carucis. f
?S&B. Land for 4 ploughs. and a jurisdiction of ` Sturton' and Kirkby. 9 Freemen and 6 smallholders with 3 ploughs.
Soca In Waldingurde. VI. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. carucas. Soca. est in Stratone et Cherchebi. Ibi XX. sochemanni cum IIII. carucis. et XX. acrae prati. et XXV. acrae siluae minutae
In WADDINGWORTH 6 carucates of land taxable. Land for 6 ploughs. It is a jurisdiction of ` Sturton' and of Kirkby. 20 Freemen with 4 ploughs. Meadow, 20 acres; underwood, 25 acres.

Manerium In CHIRCHEBI. habuit Haroldus. X. bouatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad XIII. boues. Ibi habet episcopus dunelmensis. III. uillanos et II. bordarios cum dimidia caruca. et XII. acrae prati. et CLXX. acrae siluae pastilis per loca. T. R. E. ualuit C. solidos. modo similiter.

In KIRKBY Harold had 10 bovates of land taxable. Land for 13 oxen. The Bishop of Durham has 3 villagers and 2 smallholders with 1/2 plough. Meadow, 12 acres; pasturable woodland in places, 170 acres. Value before 1066, 100s; now the same.

In Martone. est soca huius Manerii. XII. bouatae ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi. III. sochemanni et II. bordarii habent. I. carucam. et XXX. IIII. acrae prati. et XXX. III. acrae silue pastilis. et LX. acrae siluae minutae.

In MARTIN there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 12 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. 3 Freemen and 2 smallholders have 1 plough. Meadow, 34 acres; pasturable woodland, 33 acres; underwood, 60 acres. . Harold had it

In Torp. est inland et soca eiusdem Manerii. II. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi XVI. uillani et I. sochemannus et IIII. bordarii habent. I. carucam. et terciam partem. II. molendinorum. de. VIII. solidis. et III. K piscarias de. VII. solidis et VI. denariis. et XV. acrae prati. et CXX. acrae siluae pastilis. Eudo calumniatur.

In THORPE there is and a jurisdiction also of this manor; 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. 16 villagers, 1 Freeman and 4 smallholders have 1 plough and the third part of 2 mills at 8s; 3 fisheries at 7s 6d; meadow, 15 acres; pasturable woodland, 120 acres. Eudo claims it. . Harold had it

Manerium In COUENHAM. habuit Esbern. II. carucatas terre et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas et VI. boues. Nunc |de episcopo habet Sanctus Karilefus ibi. II. carucas. et dimidiam. et XII. uillanos. et VI. sochemannos cum. I. caruca et dimidia. Ibi aecclesia. et LX. acrae prati. et II. saline. III. solidorum. T. R. E. ualuit. LX. solidos $ modo. IIII. libras. Tailla. XX. solidos.

In COVENHAM Esbiorn had 2 1/2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs and 6 oxen. Now Saint-Calais has 2 1/2 ploughs there from the Bishop. 12 villagers and 6 Freemen with 1 1/2 ploughs. A church; meadow, 60 acres; 2 salt-houses, 3s. Value before 1066, 60s; now £4. Exactions 20s.
In Grimesbi. est soca huius Manerii. II. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. III. boues. Wasta fuit. modo colitur.
In GRIMSBY there is a jurisdiction of this manor, 2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 3 oxen. It was waste; it is now cultivated. [Jurisdiction of Covenham. Esbiorn had it. Saint-Calais has it

. II. Maneria In FOREBI. habuerunt Summerled et Archil VII. bouatas ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas et V. boues. Ibi Turstinus homo episcopi habet. I. sochemannum. VI. uillanos habentes. I. carucam. et XLII. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo. XX. solidos.

In FOTHERBY Sumarlithi and Arnketil had 7 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs and 5 oxen. , the Bishop's man, has 1 Freeman; 6 villagers who have 1 plough. Meadow, 42 acres. Value before 1066 £4; now 20s.

Manerium In ENDREBI et Radebi habuit Elnod. IIII. bouatas et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. V. boues. Nunc. est dimidia caruca ibi. et I. uillanus. et dimidia sedes molendini. et X. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. X. solidos. modo similiter.
In ENDERBY and RAITHBY Alnoth had 4 1/2 bovates taxable. Land for 5 oxen. Now 1/2 plough there. 1 villager. 1/2 mill site; meadow, 10 acres. Value before 1066, 10s; now the same.
In Hundelbi. IIII. acras terrae.
In HUNDLEBY 4 acres of land.

Manerium In SPILESBI et Iresbi et Torp. habuit Aschil. VI. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. carucas. Ibi habet episcopus. I. carucam. et V. uillanos et V. sochemannos. et I. bordarium. habentes. I. carucam. et II. molendinum. IX K solidorum. et XII. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. solidos. modo similiter. Eudo /clamat.

In SPILSBY, ERESBY and THORPE Eskil had 6 carucates of land taxable. Land for 6 ploughs. The Bishop has 1 plough. 5 villagers, 5 Freemen and 1 smallholder who have 1 plough. 2 mills, 9s; meadow, 12 acres. Value before 1066, 20s; now the same. Eudo claims it.
Ibidem. est soca. de Gredbi. et Estrecale. II. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Ibi XII. sochemanni habent. II. carucas.
There also is a jurisdiction of Grebby and East Keal; 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. 12 Freemen have 2 ploughs.

Manerium In ESTRECALE. habuit Elnod. VI. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. boues. Ibi. II. uillani arant. II. bobus. Ibi. V. acrae K prati. T. R. E. ualuit X. solidos. modo similiter. Eudo clamat socam.
In EAST KEAL Alnoth had 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 6 oxen. 2 villagers plough with 2 oxen. Meadow, 5 acres. Value before 1066, 10s; now the same. Eudo claims the jurisdiction.
